Table 2: Comparison with the state-of-the-art methods on Nr3D in terms of different evaluation metrics.
Figure 5: Failure cases of our framework under severe occlusion and heavy view truncation.

Figure 1: Comparisons with zero-shot 3DVG methods. Compared with SeeGround [14] and SeqVLM [15] that passively rely on noisy 3D proposals or perform exhaustive reasoning over redundant multi-view projections, MCM-VG explicitly enforces rigorous 2D-3D consistency at the semantic, instance, and viewpoint levels, thereby improving both grounding accuracy and reasoning reliability.

Table 3: Ablation studies of different components and strategies on ScanRefer. SA, IR, and VD denote the Semantic Alignment Module, the Instance Rectification Module, and the Viewpoint Distillation Module, respectively.
Figure 6: More visualization results of 3D visual grounding on the ScanRefer [5] dataset.
Figure 3: Visualization results of 3D visual grounding on the ScanRefer[5] dataset.

Figure 2: Overview of the MCM-VG pipeline for zero-shot 3D visual grounding. MCM-VG contains three modules for comprehensive 2D-3D mappings: (a) Semantic Alignment Module to correct semantic mismatches, (b) Instance Rectification Module to rectify instance localization, and (c) Viewpoint Distillation Module to distill the optimal viewpoint for final VLM reasoning.
